title: Cross-Faculty Statisticians’ Meeting 2026
text:
This meeting aims to bring together statisticians from across different faculties at King’s College London.
The workshop is co-organised by Dr Vasiliki Koutra, Prof Richard Emsley, Prof Daniel Stahl, and Dr Lauren Bell.
The meeting is an opportunity to share current statistical work and applications, and identify common interests to help shape follow-up interactions.

title: Cross-Faculty Statisticians’ Meeting 2026
text:

title: Cross-Faculty Statisticians’ Meeting 2026
text:
Location






The workshop will be held in the Franklin-Wilkins building on the Waterloo Campus. The main entrance is on Stamford Street.
All scientific sessions are in Room FWB 1.60. Check-in, coffee, lunch and tea are in the Glass Suites.

title: Cross-Faculty Statisticians’ Meeting 2026
text:
Programme












11:00 - 11:30 Check-in & coffee








11:30 - 11:35 Welcome & introduction (Vasiliki Koutra)








11:35 - 13:00 Session 1 (Chair: Daniel Stahl)


Vasiliki Koutra
Experimental design for interference


Mollie Payne
A novel trial design for dose-optimisation in complex interventions


Steven Gilmour
A Critical Review of the Literature on Factorial Clinical Trials


Gordon Forbes
Will work as a clinical trial statistician be fully automated by an AI within the next 12 to 18 months?


13:00 - 14:00 Lunch buffet








14:00 - 15:30 Session 2 (Chair: Lauren Bell)


Marina Riabiz
A review of modern Bayesian and post-Bayesian frameworks and sampling methods


Yanwen Zheng
Oral Health, Chronic Obstructive Pulmonary Disease and All-Cause Mortality


Kalliopi Mylona
Optimal experimental designs in pharmaceutical manufacturing


Jeremy Brown 
Applied causal inference with target trial emulation for estimating the effects of chronic medications in pregnancy


Yu Luo
Bayesian methods for some medical applications


15:30 - 16:00 Tea break








16:00 - 16:30 Session 3 (Chair: Richard Emsley)


Round table discussion
How can statisticians at King's raise our profile and work more closely together?


16:30 Close of the day
